steps.title: Find the slope of the linear function
steps.content:
The function is given as $$T(x)=67-8x$$, which is in the form $$y=mx+b$$ with slope $$m=-8$$.

The slope tells us how much T changes when x increases by 1 hour.

Because the slope is negative, the temperature drops $$|m|$$ degrees each hour.
Therefore the estimated decrease is 8°F per hour.
That matches choice B — B is correct.

steps.title: Verify with two specific hours
steps.content:
Compute the temperature at x=1 and x=2: $$T(1)=67-8(1)=59$$ and $$T(2)=67-8(2)=51$$. The change is $$T(2)-T(1)=51-59=-8$$, so the temperature falls 8 degrees when x increases by 1 hour.
This confirms the decrease per hour is 8°F.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
